enriched_points.expanded_content: The first USDA-approved plant-made vaccine, derived from tobacco cells, effectively protects poultry against Newcastle disease, showcasing the potential of plants in veterinary immunization.

Plant-based systems are explored for producing recombinant glucocerebrosidase, the enzyme deficient in Gaucher’s disease, offering a cost-effective and scalable alternative to traditional production methods.
Plants have been engineered to produce insulin, providing a sustainable and efficient production method that could meet increasing global diabetes treatment demands.
